commit.message:
fix: Surface Go display — disable PSR, DC, Plymouth + multiple safe modes

GRUB now has 4 entries:
1. Default: i915.enable_psr=0 + i915.enable_dc=0 + plymouth.enable=0
2. Safe Mode (basic): nomodeset for totally broken GPU
3. Safe Mode (Intel forced): i915.modeset=1 with PSR/DC disabled
4. Install to Disk: same as default + installer

Key fix: plymouth.enable=0 prevents splash screen from stealing
the framebuffer and going black. Also removed 'splash' parameter.

commit.message:
fix: auto-merge detection and recovery for Builder worktree isolation (#45)

* Initial plan

* fix: add auto-merge detection and recovery for Builder worktree isolation

Three field-test observations showed isolation="worktree" sometimes
auto-merges the Builder's output without Architect inspection (3 of 8
dispatches). The skill promised isolation but couldn't detect or
recover from the tool-level inconsistency.

Changes:
- generation/SKILL.md: adds State A (worktree-live) / State B
  (auto-merged) detection to Verification section with explicit
  recovery path for State B (git diff inspect → accept or revert+redispatch)
- takeover/SKILL.md: replaces "Worktree merges automatically" with
  reference to the generation skill's Verification protocol, removing
  the contradictory implication that no validation step exists

* fix: clean new files during auto-merge recovery (State B revert)

git checkout HEAD only reverts files that exist in HEAD. New files
created by the Builder survive and pollute the tree for the next
dispatch. Add git clean -f for Builder-created files that don't
exist in HEAD.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all PR #45 review findings -- naming, ground-truth, recovery

Addresses 2 critical + 4 important + 3 medium findings:

Critical:
1. Rename "State A/B" to "worktree-live/auto-merged" to avoid
   collision with "Stage A/B" (Architect/Builder)
2. Use git diff HEAD --name-only for ground-truth file detection
   instead of Builder's self-reported list. Add git ls-files for
   new untracked files.

Important:
3. Add auto-merged handling to BLOCKED/failure path (was only in DONE)
4. Detect committed vs working-tree auto-merge via git status
5. Remove no-op AUTO_MERGE_DETECTED log, replace with structured
   diagnostic cache entry using existing .unslop/last-failure/ lifecycle
6. Revert Protocol handles pre-existing dirty state (re-stage spec
   after checkout), clears index via git reset HEAD

Medium:
- Timestamp check includes "older than session start time"
- Diagnostic cache format defined for auto-merge reverts
- Takeover forward reference made resilient (describes steps, not heading)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(output): fall back to show-option for tmux < 3.3a set-clipboard

#{set-clipboard} format variable is only available since tmux 3.3a (Sep 2022).
On older tmux (Ubuntu 22.04: 3.2a, RHEL 9: 3.2a, Debian 11: 3.1c), it returns
an empty string, causing the previous code to incorrectly fall back to "on"
even when the user had set-clipboard off.

Fix: extract parse_display_message() as a pure function returning Option for the
set-clipboard field. When None (empty response), fall back to
`tmux show-option -gv set-clipboard`, which works on all tmux versions.

Adds 5 unit tests for parse_display_message covering: on, off, external,
empty (< 3.3a), and no-pid cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.
